chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No.D-4068 of 2025 ____________________________________________________________ Date Order with Signature of Judge(s) ____________________________________________________________ Bilal Hussain & others..............................................Petitioners Versus Federation of Pakistan and others......................................Respondents Date of hearing:29.08.2025 Date of order:29.08.2025 Mr. Ramiz Naseem, Advocate for the Petitioners. Mr. Pervez Ahmed Mastoi, AAG Syed Bashir Hussain Shah, Asstt. Attorney General. DSP Ayaz Ahmed Pandhiani & SIP Jawaid Akhtar, FRO/SB Karachi. ----------------------- Learned Asstt. Attorney General files parawise comments on behalf of respondents No.2 & 4, the same are taken on record, copies whereof have been provided to the learned counsel appearing for the petitioners. It is evident from the comments furnished that the noted respondents have no objection to the grant of the instant petition subject to certain formalities enumerated therein. By consent, instant petition is disposed of in the following terms:- 1) The petitioners shall obtain travel permits from the High Commission of Bangladesh, Karachi. The same shall be verified from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs for confirmation of their genuineness. 2) Petitioners are allowed to travel subject to further verification of their status, criminal record, CRO/CRI. 3) The Director General, NADRA Sindh is directed to verify the identity of the petitioners through biometric for NADRA registration. If the 2 petitioners are registered in NADRA their CNICs may be cancelled/blocked. 4) The Director General, NADRA Sindh is further directed to provide certificate to the petitioners subject to the above. 5) After completion of all codal formalities as required under the Foreign Act, 1946, the Petitioners may be permitted to travel to their native country Bangladesh.

JUDGE JUDGE Nadeem
